Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

I agree that making the code more robust is sensible, but it is REALLY strange that kmalloc would return non-aligned buffers.  I'm sure other code assumes that.
Maybe this memory comes from somewhere else...

Patch Set 1:

AFAIU, kmalloc() itself has no strict requirement to align chunks always, it is not something it MUST guarantee but usually that is what SLAB allocator does. I am using SLUB locally for variety and that looks like it packs chunks more aggressively to save memory and producing more non-aligned chunks as result.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-12251 tests: stop running sanity-flr for PPC

commit.subject: LU-13004 target: take offset into account in tgt_send_buffer
commit.message:
LU-13004 target: take offset into account in tgt_send_buffer

While calculating amount of pages needed, take buffer offset into
account because it can be non-aligned after allocations in
out_read().

Signed-off-by: Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ib7c9b35d328d366a27cc553ffe2f2c5930949cf4
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/37571
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Neil Brown <neilb@suse.de>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
